text:
Market today: Sentiment is cautiously constructive on AI hardware - the 'follow the money' narrative (Nvidia, SK Hynix, compute infrastructure) is reinforcing what you already own. Oil weakness and macro noise are background; your world is chips and tech, and the bid there feels steady but not aggressive.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest alarm - a 3X leveraged semiconductor ETF sitting 14.5% underwater, and your stop at $200 is close. On margin, a leveraged ETF decaying against you is a double tax. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, and already down nearly 14% with a stop just below current price at $21.38 - margin amplifies any further drop hard. SMH (-2.5%) is mild but worth watching as your unlevered chip proxy.

Watch: Whether SOXL holds $200 (your stop) - a break accelerates fast on 3X leverage. Also watch if AI hardware names (Nvidia/TSM) catch a bid off today's 'follow the money' headlines.

Bottom line: Protect capital on your two bleeding leveraged positions before thinking about anything new.
plays: SOXL: Your stop is $200 - honor it without negotiation. A 3X leveraged ETF down 14.5% on margin can get ugly fast; no reason to let it become a bigger hole.
SPAL: Stop is $21.38, price is $21.70 - you have almost no cushion. Decide now whether you want to be in this trade; the risk/reward at this distance to stop is razor-thin on margin.
No new buys today - your two leveraged losers need resolving first. Adding exposure while SOXL and SPAL are bleeding on margin is the wrong sequence.
